items.fromString:
*Reproduce Steps:*

We have POD 32Z430011909019C which is active in contract МПМТ-2406000211 from 01.07.2024 to 31.01.2026 (customer with identifier 4506098796 - status is LOST) and in contract МПОК-2512000711 from 01.02.2026 (customer with identifier 7710028805). The POD is included in a request for disconnection → [https://apps.energo-pro.bg/phoenix-epres/request-for-disconnection/preview/pod?id=1048|https://apps.energo-pro.bg/phoenix-epres/request-for-disconnection/preview/pod?id=1048]  and a disconnection → [https://apps.energo-pro.bg/phoenix-epres/disconnection-of-power-supply/preview?id=1134|https://apps.energo-pro.bg/phoenix-epres/disconnection-of-power-supply/preview?id=1134] with customer identifier 7710028805.

*Actual Result:*

For the old customer 4506098796  we have a label “POD is disconnected”

!{93571FFB-211C-410D-80E6-2B56779546C3}-20260518-113728.png|width=669,alt="{93571FFB-211C-410D-80E6-2B56779546C3}-20260518-113728.png"!



*Expected Result:*

We should not have a label for the old customer.

_________________

Endpoint:
Method:
Status:
Payload:
Response:

Update logic: We should not mark customers with the label "Pod is disconnected" when the customer status is "LOST".

Updated on Confluence: [Disconnection of the power supply - Edit - Phoenix - Confluence|https://asterbit.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Phoenix/pages/76054614/Disconnection+of+the+power+supply+-+Edit]



{color:#6554c0}*GE Description:*{color}

*Rule 1 — POD is active in a current contract*

If the disconnected POD is *currently active* in at least one contract, display *DISCONNECTED* on the *customer* linked to that contract.

*Rule 2 — POD is not active in any current contract*

If the disconnected POD is *not active in any contract at the moment*, display *DISCONNECTED* on the *customer* linked to the *most recent past contract* in which the POD was active.

* “Most recent” means the contract with the *latest end date* (or *latest deactivation date* of the POD on that contract) among all historical contracts where the POD was active.

{color:#6554c0}*GE Description:*{color}

*Rule 1 - POD is active in a current contract*

If the disconnected POD is *currently active* in at least one contract, display *DISCONNECTED* on the *customer* linked to that contract.

*Rule 2 - POD is not active in any current contract*

If the disconnected POD is *not active in any contract at the moment*, display *DISCONNECTED* on the *customer* linked to the *most recent past contract* in which the POD was active.

* “Most recent” means the contract with the *latest end date* (or *latest deactivation date* of the POD on that contract) among all historical contracts where the POD was active.

*Rule 3 -* We should not mark customers with the label "Pod is disconnected" when the customer status is "LOST".

hello , i can’t found any description about this case so i think it’s looks like change request.
Example with customer UIC 4506098796 and POD
32Z430011909019C on DEV. The POD are deactivated from contract EPES2509041722 and activated in contract EPES2606000005 (UIC 8312201073) from 01.02.2026. Request for disconnection for UIC 8312201075 →
and we still have label “POD is disconnected” in the old customer UIC 4506098796.
We are waiting for logic review from BG side.

The system should display the label DISCONNECTED in the customer which has a contract in which the disconnected POD is currently active. If the POD is not active in any contract at the moment then the label DISCONNECTED should be in the customer which has the latest contract in the past in which the POD was active

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.
